name: 1940s
description: World War II, the atomic age, FDR/Truman, and the decade that reshaped the world.

questions.q: What surprise attack on December 7, 1941 brought the U.S. into World War II?

source.notes: Web-verified 2026-05-09. FDR called it 'a date which will live in infamy.'

questions.q: Which three nations formed the Axis Powers in WWII?

source.notes: Tripartite Pact signed September 27, 1940.

questions.q: What was the codename for the U.S. project to develop the atomic bomb?

source.notes: Started 1939, accelerated 1942 under Gen. Leslie Groves.

questions.q: Where was the first atomic bomb successfully tested on July 16, 1945?

source.notes: Web-verified 2026-05-09. Trinity test in Jornada del Muerto desert on Alamogordo Bombing Range. Source: Wikipedia.

questions.q: Which two Japanese cities were bombed with atomic weapons in August 1945?

source.notes: Hiroshima August 6, Nagasaki August 9, 1945.

questions.q: Who was the British Prime Minister who led the UK through most of WWII?

source.notes: PM May 1940-July 1945. Chamberlain immediately preceded him.

questions.q: On what date did Allied forces land at Normandy (D-Day)?

source.notes: Operation Overlord. Largest seaborne invasion in history.

questions.q: Who was U.S. President at the start of WWII (and through most of the war)?

source.notes: Died April 12, 1945; Truman succeeded him.

questions.q: Who became U.S. President after FDR died in April 1945?

source.notes: VP since January 1945.

questions.q: When was the United Nations officially founded?

source.notes: Web-verified 2026-05-09. Charter came into force when ratified by the 5 permanent Security Council members. Source: history.state.gov.

questions.q: Which 1945 conference shaped post-war Europe with FDR, Churchill, and Stalin?

source.notes: Web-verified 2026-05-09. Held February 4-11, 1945 in Crimea. Source: Britannica.

questions.q: Which battle (1942-43) is widely considered the turning point of WWII on the Eastern Front?

source.notes: Soviets defeated 6th German Army; ~2M total casualties.

questions.q: Which date is V-E Day (Victory in Europe)?

source.notes: Germany's unconditional surrender. V-J Day was August 15.

questions.q: Who was the assassinated Indian independence leader, killed January 30, 1948?

source.notes: Web-verified 2026-05-09. Shot by Nathuram Godse at Birla House, New Delhi. Source: Britannica.

questions.q: When did Israel declare independence?

source.notes: Declaration by David Ben-Gurion. Israeli War of Independence followed.

questions.q: What 1948 airlift supplied West Berlin during Soviet blockade?
choices: Both names — official US name was Operation Vittles, popularly the Berlin Airlift

source.notes: Both accepted. UK called it Operation Plainfare.

questions.q: Which 1939 invasion is generally considered the start of WWII in Europe?

source.notes: September 1, 1939. Britain & France declared war 9/3.

questions.q: What 1947 plan provided U.S. economic aid to rebuild Europe?

source.notes: European Recovery Program. Provided ~$13B over 4 years.

questions.q: What 1947 act partitioned British India into India and Pakistan?
choices: Both — the Mountbatten Plan led to the Act

source.notes: Both partly correct. Independence August 14-15, 1947.

questions.q: Which Holocaust death camp had 'Arbeit Macht Frei' over its gates?

source.notes: 'Work sets you free' — also at Dachau and other camps. Auschwitz I most famously.
